summary: Implement Worker-authoritative pause, speed, calendar advance, and auto-pause controls
acceptance_criteria: Pause, 1x, 2x, and 4x are authoritative Worker state transitions through the established SetSpeed contract; UI labels change only after Worker acknowledgement or read-model update.
Simulation advance uses fixed deterministic steps and the authoritative calendar, never browser frame count, Date.now, setTimeout elapsed duration, or client-local counters as world time.
A normal production session can advance at least one complete in-game month and execute scheduled settlement without a debug harness or direct sim-core call.
Configured decision, error, load, reconnect, or policy conditions auto-pause deterministically with a stable reason exposed in the read model.
Invalid, stale-session, or out-of-order speed requests are rejected without changing authoritative time state.
Equivalent command streams and step budgets produce the same calendar, scheduler order, state hash, and auto-pause reason in browser Worker and Node.
notes: The Worker/protocol task must define SetSpeed before this task starts; protocol files are not writable here.
No wall-clock API may enter sim-core.
